worktrees.name: coding-88a842f2--commit-and-merge-completed-verification
worktrees.task:
Commit and merge completed verification work from a worktree back to the main repository.

The worktree at this path contains completed verification work that needs to be committed and merged back:
/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-6ed0f658--please-review-the-plan-and-intent-docume

The only new file produced in this worktree is:
projects/POC/.sessions/20260312-051840/verification-alignment.md

Steps:
1. cd into the worktree at /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-6ed0f658--please-review-the-plan-and-intent-docume
2. Run git status to confirm what changed
3. Stage the new file: git add projects/POC/.sessions/20260312-051840/verification-alignment.md
4. Commit with message: 'Add verification alignment for intent team capability changes (session 20260312-051840)'
5. Check if the project has a specific merge/close-worktree script — check /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/run.sh and /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/scripts/ for anything like close_worktree.sh or a worktree lifecycle command. Also inspect /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/scripts/worktree_manifest.py or look at existing merged worktrees for the pattern used.
6. If a close/merge script exists, use that. Otherwise, check out the main branch in the main repo and cherry-pick the commit.
7. Report the git log of what was committed and whether the merge to main succeeded.

Use absolute paths throughout. The main repo is at /Users/darrell/git/teaparty.

worktrees.name: coding-d8dfb4c8--commit-and-merge-completed-verification
worktrees.task:
Commit and merge completed verification work from a worktree back to the main repository. PLAN APPROVED by project-lead: the plan produced in session 20260312-084148 is correct — follow those steps exactly.

The worktree at this path contains completed verification work:
/Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/.worktrees/session-6ed0f658--please-review-the-plan-and-intent-docume

The only new file produced in this worktree is:
projects/POC/.sessions/20260312-051840/verification-alignment.md

Steps (from approved plan):
1. Confirm state: git -C <worktree> status and branch
2. Stage and commit: git -C <worktree> add <file> && git -C <worktree> commit -m 'Add verification alignment for intent team capability changes (session 20260312-051840)'
3. Squash-merge the session branch into main: git -C /Users/darrell/git/teaparty merge --squash <SESSION_BRANCH>
4. Commit the squash in main with the same message (only if staged changes exist)
5. Mark worktree complete: python3 /Users/darrell/git/teaparty/projects/POC/scripts/worktree_manifest.py complete --name session-6ed0f658--please-review-the-plan-and-intent-docume --repo-dir /Users/darrell/git/teaparty
6. Remove worktree and branch: git -C /Users/darrell/git/teaparty worktree remove <WORKTREE_PATH> && git -C /Users/darrell/git/teaparty branch -d <SESSION_BRANCH>
7. Report git log --oneline -5 from main repo

Use absolute paths throughout. The main repo is at /Users/darrell/git/teaparty.

worktrees.name: session-142239--the-first-page-of-the-github-d
worktrees.task: The first page of the github documents for this project is way too large.   and has multiple errors.   First, the problem that we are solving is not just context rot and scoping,   There is the problem of human escalation -- too much and the human starts rubber stamping, too little and the intent diverges.   3) importance of backtracking - when errors are discovered you need to go back sometimes to the very beginning.   This is not captured well in the plan-execute model 4) intent gap - human's arent partners in the conversation.   The agent builds what it wants to build with little understanding of what was requested.
